prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French:
"Le silence entre nous disait plus que n'importe quel argument aurait pu le faire."

Note: The phrase "any argument could have" was translated as "n'importe quel argument aurait pu le faire", which is a literal translation. An alternative phrasing could be "plus qu'un argument ne l'aurait fait", meaning "more than an argument would have done".

Japanese:
"二人の間の沈黙は、どんな議論よりも得多かった。"

Note: In Japanese, the concept of "any argument" was translated as "どんな議論" (donna igiin, "what kind of argument"), which conveys the idea of any possible argument. 

Arabic:
"صمتٌ بيننا كان أبلغ مما يمكن لأيِّ نقاشٍ أن يقوله."

Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was rendered as "أبلغ مما يمكن لأيِّ نقاشٍ أن يقوله" ('ablgh mimma yumkin li-ayy naqshshin an yaquluh), literally "more eloquent than any discussion could have said", to convey the intensity of the meaning.
